asi.hypothesis: moving shifts 1 and 5 to ALU to free VALU slots
asi.rollback_reason: ALU shift takes 8 lanes/group, becomes new bottleneck
asi.next_action_hint: ALU shifts cost 8x more slots than VALU; only shift 3 stays; try keeping (3,) and look elsewhere. Real bottleneck is load throughput (10 gather rounds × 128 = 1280 load cycles min)

description: TRUE baseline - pristine HEAD after prior revert

asi.correction: Previous 1448 baseline was from uncommitted work that was lost during discard revert. True starting point is 147734
asi.hypothesis: measure true baseline (pristine perf_takehome.py from HEAD)
asi.strategy: rebuild optimizations from scratch: vectorize with vload/vstore, multiply_add hash stages, arith-select early tree levels, scheduler for cross-round pipelining

description: Full vectorized rewrite w/ list scheduler

asi.breakdown: Vectorized with 32 groups of VLEN=8; multiply_add for hash stages 0/2/4; arith-select levels 0/1/2; gather levels 3-10; list scheduler packs VLIW bundles
asi.hypothesis: full vectorized rewrite with multiply_add + arith-select levels 0-2 + list scheduler
asi.next_ideas: 1) Move stage-3 shift to ALU to free valu; 2) Pipeline more aggressively; 3) Try level-3 arith-select if valu has slack; 4) Merge val^node into multiply_add via algebra

asi.hypothesis: stage-3 shift on ALU frees valu slot (1/group/round)
asi.note: ALU was completely idle; even 8-lane cost is offset by scheduler flexibility

asi.hypothesis: bit-extract (val&1) on ALU per-lane frees 1-2 valu ops per group per round

description: Move stage-5 shift to ALU too

asi.hypothesis: move stage-5 shift to ALU also
asi.rollback_reason: +27 cycles; stage-5 ALU shift causes scheduling interference with bit-extract (both compete for 12 alu/cycle)
asi.next_action_hint: keep stage-5 on valu; try stage-1 alternative or different direction

asi.hypothesis: move stage-1 shift to ALU (not stage 5) — stage 1 is earlier in chain, better for scheduler
sort by priority first, then engine
asi.note: identical; priorities dominated the existing tiebreaker

asi.hypothesis: move setup const/tree loads into scheduler so they overlap with broadcasts
asi.note: smaller than expected gain — loads weren't fully idle, or critical path still elsewhere

asi.hypothesis: move level-2 bit extracts to per-lane ALU
asi.rollback_reason: +27 cycles; 8x alu cost outweighs valu savings for only 2 rounds

description: Level-2 algebraic refactor with ALU bits

asi.hypothesis: algebraic refactor of level-2 select + ALU bit extract
asi.note: regressed; the 3-mad chain has deeper critical path than the tree form (original had 2 parallel mads r0/r1, then 1 sub+mad). Algebraic form is strictly sequential.

asi.bug_hypothesis: gather bc for round N+1 writes g_node which might be needed by round N's hash x0 if level>=1; but I tracked node_war = [x0 of prev]. Somewhere WAR is missing
asi.hypothesis: decouple gather bc deps from prev hash completion
asi.next_action_hint: need more careful tracking; maybe t1/t2 WAR or val WAR is the actual issue
asi.rollback_reason: correctness failure (215 diffs starting at group 2)

asi.hypothesis: decouple gather bc from p_up dep
asi.note: slightly slower. Priority change from dep reduction seemingly shifted scheduling in a bad direction. Dep reduction alone doesn't help because critical path through hash wasn't loosened.

asi.bug: aliasing g_t2=g_node breaks level-2 sel which reads both node and t2 in mad(node, t1, t2, node) — same scratch read twice corrupts result
asi.hypothesis: alias g_t2 onto g_node to save 256 scratch words for level-3 arith-select
asi.next_action_hint: could alias g_t2 to g_t1 only for hash stages (where t1/t2 used at different times) but keep them distinct for level-2

asi.hypothesis: prefer ops with lower ASAP (earlier-in-program) to drive early-round work first
h3a add on ALU per-lane
asi.rollback_reason: ALU becomes bottleneck; 8x cost not worth 1 valu op savings

asi.hypothesis: track x0-based node_war fence; relax gather bc dep
asi.note: no meaningful gain. Critical path is elsewhere - probably the hash chain itself.

asi.hypothesis: move stage-5 shift to ALU while keeping stages 1,3 on ALU
asi.rollback_reason: +103 cycles; all three shifts on ALU oversaturates ALU engine
asi.verified: earlier attempt (2 stages on ALU) also regressed

asi.hypothesis: priority + descendants count + engine
asi.note: identical. Scheduler ordering not the bottleneck.

asi.hypothesis: compute g_val_ptr via flow add_imm to free 32 load slots for gather

description: Move tree/gather base addr to flow

asi.hypothesis: move more addresses to flow add_imm
asi.note: no change; load engine already wasn't bottleneck in prolog after previous flow change

description: In-place shifts (h1b/h3b/h5b -> val) w/ strict deps

asi.hypothesis: in-place shifts for h1b/h3b/h5b (writing to val)
asi.learning: scheduler can place h1b before h1a in different cycles; forcing strict dep (h1b after h1a) loses same-cycle parallelism; -10 cycles without the strict dep is unsafe (correctness fails); with strict dep, +10 cycles
asi.rollback_reason: no scheduling scheme makes this a net win

description: Split val/p dep chains (level-0 no pdep on node)

asi.hypothesis: separate val-chain vs p-chain deps; level-0 p_up now includes pdeps for WAW protection
asi.learning: previous failure was missing level-0 p_up WAW dep on prev p_up

asi.hypothesis: track x0 per round + separate val/p/node-war dep chains
asi.learning: x0 is the last read of g_node; gather bc for next round can start after prev x0 instead of prev final_val, saving many cycles of serialization

asi.hypothesis: level-3 bit extracts on per-lane ALU frees major valu pressure (5 &-ops per group per L3 round)
asi.next: look at profile for new bottleneck
